rulings.Case Title: JOHN LIPTROT VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing the Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 6, 2019, line 1. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of a reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: DANNY BLACK ET AL VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - Parker-Hannifin Corporations Notice Of Motions And Motions For Summary Adjudication Of The False Representation And Intentional Tort Causes Of Action And Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing the Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 6, 2019, line 2. *** Tentative Ruling - Part 1 of 2 *** Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Considering all evidence and inferences in favor of Plaintiffs, Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp. failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding Plaintiffs' third cause of action, fourth cause of action, and claim for punitive damages. (CCP § 437c(p)(2); Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 843, 850-51.) The burden of production "should not shift without stringent review of the direct, circumstantial and inferential evidence." (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 83.) Defendant cannot rely on Plaintiffs' factually devoid discovery responses in the absence of conducting comprehensive discovery. (Id. at 83; Aguilar, supra, 25 Cal.4th at 854-855.) Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Plaintiffs' third cause of action for false representation and fourth cause of action for intentional tort under Civil Code sections 1708, 1709, 1710. Even if Plaintiffs' discovery responses were factually devoid, Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp. failed to conduct comprehensive discovery as to either Plaintiffs' third cause of action or fourth cause of action. Nowhere in Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp.'s special interrogatories to plaintiffs, set one does Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp. ask about Plaintiffs' false representation claim or the intentional tort claim. (See Decl. Bassi, Ex. B, Ex. C., Ex. D., Ex. E.) Moreover, Defendant Parker Hannifin Corp. failed to address Plaintiffs' third and fourth causes of action in their request for production of documents. (See Decl. Bassi, Ex. F, Ex. G.) Nor did Defendant Parker Hannifin Corp. ask questions of Mr. Black as to his claim of false representation or his claims under Civil Code sections 1708, 1709, 1710. (See Decl. Bassi, Ex. H.) The Court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered." (See Scheiding, supra, 69 Cal.App.4th at 81.) Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication as to Plaintiffs' claims for punitive damages is denied. A claim for punitive damages under Civil Code section 3294 requires a showing that Parker-Hannifin Corp. carried on despicable conduct with an awareness of the probable dangerous consequences, and willfully failed to avoid such consequences. (See Pfeifer v. John Crane (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299-1301[citing Angie M. v. Superior Court (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 1217, 1228].) Mr. Black claims he was exposed to Defendant Parker Hannifin Corp. and Sacomo-Sierra's asbestos-containing products from the 1960s to the 1980s. (Decl. Bassi, Ex. C at p. 5.) Deposition testimony of Peter Sagues establishes that Sacomo-Sierra knew that asbestos was harmful to its employees in the 1970s due to OSHA regulations. (Decl. Bassi, Ex. L at p. 140:8-20, 142:11-143:10, Ex. M at p. 172:13-176:9.) The evidence demonstrates that during the relevant time period, Sacomo-Sierra likely new that their asbestos-containing products were dangerous, but willfully failed to avoid the danger. The Court disregards Parker-Hannifin Corp.'s Exhibit Q to the declaration of Mr. Bassi because the purported documents were required to be filed electronically. (See CCP § 1010.6(d); see CRC, rule 2.253(b); see SF Local Rule 2.11H(1); see CRC, rule 3.1350(g).) *** Tentative Ruling Continues in part 2 of 2 ***

rulings.Case Title: DANNY BLACK ET AL VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing the Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 6, 2019, line 2. Defendant R.F. MacDonald Co.'s motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant R.F. MacDonald Co. failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that Defendant R.F. MacDonald Co. acted with the requisite malice, fraud, or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (See Decl. Yuen, Ex. B, Ex. C, Ex. D.) The burden of production "should not shift without stringent review of the direct, circumstantial and inferential evidence." (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 83.) Objections raised during discovery "are not the equivalent of a factually devoid response, and therefore cannot be used to shift the burden from the defendant to the plaintiff under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivision (p)(2)." (Gaggero v. Yura (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 884, 893.) Even if Plaintiffs' discovery responses are factually devoid, Defendant R.F. MacDonald Co. did not conduct comprehensive discovery as to punitive damages. Defendant cannot rely on Plaintiffs' factually devoid discovery responses in the absence of conducting comprehensive discovery. (Id. at 83; Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 854-855.) The Court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered." (See Scheiding, supra, 69 Cal.App.4th at 81.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
